current_step: T-015 closed. M2 (T-007 .. T-015) closed honestly per 'no debt across milestones' discipline. Compaction worker per S3.1 §11.5 / §12 / §13 landed. New crates/aios-evidence/src/compaction.rs (~600 LoC, 19 unit tests). Closed CompactionPolicy (Auto / OperatorApproval default per §11.5 / Disabled — serde wire tokens lowercase exact). CompactionWorker with sync tick(&mut self, backend, now) primitive; approve_segment(&SegmentId) for out-of-band operator authorisation; production wraps in tokio::time::interval task (documented in module header; not shipped). CompactionReport with eligible/compacted/awaiting/receipts_removed/index_entries_pruned counts (Serialize for §20 telemetry). CompactionBackend trait (list_sealed_segments + compact_segment + emit_approval_required) implemented for both backends. InMemoryEvidenceLog: new sealed_segments std::sync::Mutex<SealedStore>; new seal_current_segment(retention_class) method; warm_receipt_count/warm_index_count zero on compact (tombstone preserved §12); emit_approval_required appends through normal signed+chained pipeline via tokio blocking_write. RocksDbEvidenceLog: list scans chain_index→segments CFs (sync DB read); compact_segment one atomic WriteBatch (delete receipts CF rows + record_type_index CF rows; set compacted_at::<seg_id> sentinel in metadata CF — segment row in segments CF untouched so recover_sealed_chain on reopen continues to verify); emit_approval_required uses blocking_write on open-segment state; helper rocksdb_segment_compacted_at(db, seg_id) introspects sentinel. list_sealed_segments in BOTH backends filters out already-compacted segments so the worker does not re-emit approval-required every tick (operator UX + chain noise). RecordType +1 constitutional addition: CompactionApprovalRequired (wire ID 1000 — first entry in reserved Wave 14+ range; retention FOREVER per §11.5). Proto IDL: COMPACTION_APPROVAL_REQUIRED = 1000; reserved 1001 to 9999. Total RecordType variants = 428 (Wave 13 dense 1..=427 + Wave 14+ reserved 1 = 428). 6+ invariant tests updated to split Wave 13 (dense) from Wave 14+ (reserved). New encoding helper metadata_compacted_at_key returning the metadata-CF key; compaction state OUT of segments CF on purpose (preserves §11.4 cryptographic recovery byte-layout). Privacy ceiling: CompactionApprovalRequired added to is_constitutionally_public_record_type — operators always see compaction governance events. lib.rs re-exports: CompactionPolicy, CompactionWorker, CompactionReport, CompactionBackend, COMPACTION_WORKER_SUBJECT, is_compaction_eligible_for. Tests: 19 in compaction.rs + 6 in impl_inmemory.rs + 5 in rocksdb_backend.rs + 3 in tests/compaction_lifecycle.rs = +33 net (some prior tests adjusted for new field). Workspace 330 → 364 (+34). aios-evidence Cargo.toml version 0.0.1 → 0.1.0 (M2 closure marker). All gates: cargo build --workspace + cargo test --workspace (364 PASS) + cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (PASS) + cargo fmt --all -- --check (PASS).

Closed CompactionPolicy + CompactionWorker + CompactionReport + CompactionBackend trait. Both InMemoryEvidenceLog and RocksDbEvidenceLog implement the trait. Total variants 428 (Wave 13 dense 1..=427 + Wave 14+ reserved 1). 6+ invariant tests updated (split Wave 13 dense from Wave 14+ reserved). +33 tests net. T-015 = 9 tasks complete.
discovered_constraints_T015: CompactionBackend trait is intentionally synchronous; both backends' impls bridge to tokio-locked state via blocking_write()/blocking_read(). Production drives the worker from a spawn_blocking task; the integration test uses tokio::task::spawn_blocking. Documented in compaction.rs module header.

compact_segment MUST preserve the sealed-segment metadata row in the segments CF — the chain witness (seal hash + Ed25519 signature) is what keeps the cross-segment chain verifiable on reopen. Compaction state lives in a SEPARATE metadata CF sentinel `compacted_at::<segment_id>` so the segment-row byte layout is byte-identical to its pre-compaction form (§11.4 cryptographic recovery is untouched).

list_sealed_segments in both backends filters out already-compacted segments — otherwise OperatorApproval mode would re-emit COMPACTION_APPROVAL_REQUIRED every tick for already-compacted segments (operator UX disaster + chain pollution). The in-memory backend uses warm_receipt_count > 0 as the predicate; the RocksDB backend uses rocksdb_segment_compacted_at(...).is_none().

RecordType vocabulary expansion (Wave 13 dense 1..=427 + Wave 14+ reserved 1000..=9999) required splitting the wire_id_ranges test into two: wave_13_id_range_is_contiguous_and_dense (asserts 1..=427 stays dense) + wave_14_plus_ids_live_in_the_reserved_block (asserts every id > 427 is in 1000..=9999). The TOTAL_RECORD_TYPE_VARIANTS = WAVE_13_VARIANTS + WAVE_14_PLUS_VARIANTS bookkeeping pattern is the constitutional anchor for future Wave 14+ additions.

Proto3 reserved-range tightening: changed `reserved 1000 to 9999` to `COMPACTION_APPROVAL_REQUIRED = 1000` + `reserved 1001 to 9999`. The reserved-range overlap with a defined value is a proto3 compile error; this split is the standard pattern.

Auto-mode test against InMemoryEvidenceLog::seal_current_segment(retention_class) — note that the open-chain receipts (3 segments × N receipts) are sealed INTO three SealedSegment envelopes which carry their own retention_class. The CompactionWorker then sees one FOREVER segment + two STANDARD_24M segments; FOREVER never compacts regardless of wall-clock (is_compaction_eligible enforces this at the eligibility predicate, not at the policy level).

Sealed segment receipts() returns the original appended receipts PLUS the terminal SEGMENT_SEALED witness minted by Segment::seal(). The witness is NOT in the receipts CF (it's only in the SealedSegment envelope on the segments CF). For RocksDB compact_segment, the WriteBatch iterates ALL receipts in the envelope including the witness — the witness's delete is a no-op on the receipts CF but the count returned reflects the iteration. Test assertions skip the witness when verifying ReadReceipt round-trips against appended receipts.

Worker is purely sync + holds no Arc<Backend> reference. Tests construct one InMemoryEvidenceLog::clone() into the spawn_blocking closure and call tick(&mut backend, now) directly. Backend mutations are visible across clones because the underlying sealed_segments is Arc<Mutex<...>> (shared) and the chain state is Arc<RwLock<...>> (shared).
notes: M2 closed honestly with T-015. 9 tasks T-007..T-015 complete. Workspace 364 tests. aios-evidence at 0.1.0. M3 candidate: capability runtime (S10.1) — first end-to-end consumer of aios-action + aios-evidence through the Capability Runtime gRPC surface per S3.1 §13 lifecycle.
